title: Add FIPS mode secret
body:
If the host is in FIPS mode and /etc/system-fips exists
/run/secrets/system-fips is created in the container so that
the container can run in FIPS mode as well.

Moving the changes of https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/603 here. The secrets pkg will be vendored into buildah and cri-o to reduce code duplication.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: Do we need to have a separate set of RO bind mounts for this? That would not be hard to do, but the secrets code would have to sort them into RO/RW for us to do that.
Adds functionality for https://trello.com/c/UawSMhdW/572-openssl-fips-mode-in-containers on the libpod side.

I guess I could see making these directories read/only if the container is running read-only.  But traditionally we have allowed the secrets directory to be modified by the container, similarly to how we allow the /etc/resolv.conf and /etc/hosts to be modified.

> I guess I could see making these directories read/only if the container is running read-only. But traditionally we have allowed the secrets directory to be modified by the container, similarly to how we allow the /etc/resolv.conf and /etc/hosts to be modified.

@rhatdan if this is the case, then shouldn't `/run/secrets` be a tmpfs mount as well for the read-only case to allow the container to be able to modify it? If it is not, the containers will fail when it tries to volume mount the secrets.
@umohnani8 Could you try this out and see what happens?
@rhatdan yup will do. This looks ready to merge, PTAL.

:pushpin: Commit 622b60c has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 622b60cee3b53124ac14b7a424db282772213377 -->
@umohnani8 Now we need the same code in BUILDAH and CRI-O.
